goal: 2026-06-05-session-sources: I2 - Add source-aware storage and coverage

summary.title: Add source-aware storage and coverage
summary.what: Implemented the I2 source-aware storage slice: selector canonicalization now carries source, SQLite sessions/messages/coverage migrate to source-qualified identity, query/read/list/stats paths default to Codex source, and FTS cleanup uses rowid/session_id instead of bare session_uuid.
summary.why: The multi-source foundation needs source_id as a storage, selector, coverage, query, and read dimension before I3 can safely expose CLI --source codex behavior without cross-source collisions or destructive Codex migration shortcuts.
summary.user_goal: 2026-06-05-session-sources: I2 - Add source-aware storage and coverage

decisions.chose: Canonicalize missing selector source to codex and make selectorImplies false across sources.
decisions.rationale: D1 requires Codex compatibility while ensuring coverage from one source cannot satisfy another source.

decisions.chose: Add source_id, native_session_id, and session_key; replace global session_uuid/file_path uniqueness with source-aware uniqueness.
decisions.rationale: The same native id or raw path can exist in separate sources without sharing internal identity.

decisions.chose: Backfill old rows as Codex and preserve old messages during table rebuild by temporarily disabling FK cascade only for the migration step.
decisions.rationale: Existing Codex data must survive and continue to be readable; deleting rows and forcing resync was explicitly forbidden.

decisions.chose: Default no-selector query/list/stats to Codex, resolve reads through source-qualified session rows, and delete FTS rows by rowid/session_id.
decisions.rationale: Bare UUID behavior remains Codex-compatible while future source-qualified ids avoid native-id collisions.

rejected.reason: I3 owns public CLI source option behavior and unsupported-source error shape.

rejected.alternative: Drop or rebuild all existing Codex data as a shortcut
rejected.reason: The task explicitly forbids deleting existing Codex data; migration/backfill is required.

rejected.alternative: Keep FTS cleanup keyed by bare session_uuid
rejected.reason: Focused tests showed it deletes another source's FTS rows when native ids collide.

review_notes: Actual I2 commit is d36fb22758008068420fa0133683ac76357129e9; seal prepare reports the broader branch stack against origin/main because this branch starts from the I1 workflow integration commit.
Validation: npm run check passed with 27 test files and 148 tests; git diff --check passed; checkout CLI smoke passed for sync/find/read-page/stats.
Migration compatibility evidence: old sessions plus old messages schema preserved message rows, backfilled source identity as codex, and restored SQLite foreign_keys=1.
Boundary scan: no src/cli.ts --source option and no Claude parser/public source wiring added.

architectural_claims: source_id is now a selector, coverage, DB, query, and read dimension for the I2 core path.
Codex remains the default source when public callers omit source input.
Bare read ids resolve as Codex; source-qualified internal refs can resolve non-Codex rows without changing CLI commands.
behavioral_changes: Canonical selector JSON returned by core paths now includes source: codex.
find/list/stats without a selector read Codex-scoped rows only.
read-range/read-page use source-qualified session row identity before loading messages.
prune/delete/replace operations are scoped by source.

api_changes.signature: canonicalizeSelector()/parseSelectorJson() return selectors with source defaulted to codex
getSessionRecord/read helpers resolve bare ids as Codex and source:id internally

data_model_changes.name: sessions.source_id/native_session_id/session_key
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Old rows backfill as codex, native_session_id=session_uuid, session_key=codex:<uuid>.

data_model_changes.name: sessions uniqueness
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Global session_uuid/file_path uniqueness replaced with source-aware unique constraints.

data_model_changes.name: messages uniqueness
data_model_changes.migration_notes: messages uniqueness is now session_id plus seq so native ids can collide across sources.

data_model_changes.name: coverage.source_id
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Old coverage rows canonicalize missing source to codex.

security_implications: No Claude Code parsing, public sync, docs, CLI option, release, global skill, or installed CLI behavior was added.
Source-scoped predicates reduce accidental cross-source reads/deletes once future private rows exist.
migration_notes: INDEX_VERSION bumped to cxs-v7-source-identity so old Codex rows are not treated as fresh unchanged rows.
Old Codex sessions and messages are preserved during migration; manual FK simulation confirmed foreign_keys is restored to 1.
